title: LACA
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: The Local Access to Courts Act makes it easier for people to go to federal court by adding new meeting places. The law says federal courts in Texas can now meet in College Station, which is part of the Southern District of Texas. It also says federal courts in California can now meet in El Centro, which is part of the Southern District of California. This gives people more options for where they can attend their court cases without having to travel as far.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Local Access to Courts Act or LACA.

sections.title: Section 2 Organization of Texas District Courts
sections.text: This section changes the law about where federal courts can meet in Texas. It adds College Station as a place where certain federal district courts can hold their meetings and hearings.

sections.title: Section 3 Organization of California District Courts
sections.text: This section changes the law about where federal courts can meet in California. It adds El Centro as a place where certain federal district courts can hold their meetings and hearings, in addition to San Diego.
